ksa_text: Knowledge of AI conformity assessment and compliance lifecycle management, including conformity assessment procedures, CE marking requirements, notified body engagement, post-market surveillance obligations, and AI value chain liability triggers.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) supply chain security and risk management policies, requirements, and procedures.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

onet.top_element_label: Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

Knowledge of information technology (IT) supply chain security and risk management policies, requirements, and procedures.
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: AI Governance is a primary or secondary domain for the AI CoE Leader. This knowledge KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (c suite).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (AI Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.526). External reviewers may question whether this specific knowledge KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the AI CoE Leader versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act (Article 9), and OECD AI Principles confirm AI governance as a critical competency for responsible AI leadership.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with AI Governance appearing in job postings and role definition literature for AI CoE Leader.

decision_rationale: AI Governance knowledge KSA is within scope for the AI CoE Leader at the c suite tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
